title: Accenture: Consumers show growing trust in AI shopping agents
optimized_headline: Accenture Reports Rising Consumer Trust in AI Shopping Assistants
raw_excerpt:
Consumers are showing a willingness to let AI agents take on more shopping-related tasks, according to new research from Accenture. The company’s 2026 Consumer Pulse Research, based on a survey of 25,590 consumers across 16 countries, found that 74% of respondents would trust a personal AI agent more than their best friend to make a […]

raw_body:
Consumers are showing a willingness to let AI agents take on more shopping-related tasks, according to new research from Accenture. The company’s 2026 Consumer Pulse Research, based on a survey of 25,590 consumers across 16 countries, found that 74% of respondents would trust a personal AI agent more than their best friend to make a […]
The post Accenture: Consumers show growing trust in AI shopping agents appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Accenture's 2026 Consumer Pulse Research reveals that 74% of 25,590 surveyed consumers across 16 countries trust AI shopping agents more than their best friends. This shift indicates a growing acceptance of AI in personal decision-making, particularly in shopping. As consumers become more comfortable with AI assistance, it could reshape the retail landscape and influence how brands engage with customers.
why_it_matters: Consumers are increasingly relying on AI for shopping, which could streamline their purchasing decisions and enhance convenience.
This trend signals a broader shift in consumer behavior, suggesting that AI will play a critical role in future retail strategies and customer interactions.

lenses.eli12: More people are trusting AI to help with shopping, according to Accenture's research. In fact, 74% of those surveyed prefer AI agents over friends when making purchases. This means AI could soon be seen as a reliable shopping companion, making life easier for everyone. It matters because it shows how technology is becoming a bigger part of our everyday choices.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this research highlights a growing user need for AI-driven shopping tools. With 74% of consumers trusting AI agents more than friends, there's potential for increased efficiency in the shopping experience. Brands could consider integrating AI solutions to meet this demand, enhancing customer satisfaction while potentially lowering operational costs.
lenses.engineer: Accenture's study surveyed 25,590 consumers across 16 countries, revealing that 74% would trust AI shopping agents over friends. This suggests a significant acceptance of AI in decision-making processes. Engineers could focus on improving AI algorithms for personalized recommendations, ensuring they meet user expectations while maintaining trust and reliability in AI interactions.
